summary:
The habenular trigone has an intact blood-brain barrier while others like the subfornical organ, area postrema, and neurohypophysis do not due to their characteristic lack or reduced function of this protective mechanism. 

The habenular trigone uniquely possesses a complete blood-brain barrier whereas structures such as the subfornical organ, area postrema, and neurohypophysis are classified as circumventricular organs without full-blood brain barrier integrity.
